edges.description: Germline susceptibility can be followed by focal allele loss in vascular lesion tissue.
edges.source: Germline RASA1 and EPHB4 loss of vascular Ras suppression
edges.target: Somatic second-hit allele loss in focal lesions

edges.description: Germline RASA1-related CM-AVM susceptibility manifests as multifocal capillary malformations.
edges.source: Germline RASA1 and EPHB4 loss of vascular Ras suppression

edges.description: EPHB4-related vascular signaling disruption can manifest with Bier spots in CM-AVM2.
edges.source: Germline RASA1 and EPHB4 loss of vascular Ras suppression

edges.description: EPHB4-related vascular signaling disruption can manifest with telangiectasia in CM-AVM2.
edges.source: Germline RASA1 and EPHB4 loss of vascular Ras suppression

edges.description: Focal loss of residual pathway control promotes abnormal ERK/MAPK-driven vascular morphogenesis.
edges.source: Somatic second-hit allele loss in focal lesions

edges.description: Abnormal ERK/MAPK-driven vessel morphogenesis produces fast-flow arteriovenous malformations or fistulas in CM-AVM.
The same arteriovenous malformation mechanism can involve the cerebral vasculature, including vein of Galen malformations.
Fast-flow arteriovenous shunting can produce the Parkes Weber syndrome branch of the CM-AVM spectrum.
edges.target: Parkes Weber syndrome or limb arteriovenous shunting

nodes.description: Fast-flow arteriovenous malformations and fistulas can involve skin, soft tissue, brain, spine, and other vascular beds.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the clinical and molecular findings in RASA1 capillary malformation-arteriovenous malformation.
evidence.snippet: Most (75.4%) individuals with a RASA1 variant had CMs, and 44.9% had an AVM/AVF.

nodes.description: Bier spots are reported as a distinct cutaneous finding in EPHB4-related CM-AVM2.

evidence.reference_title: Capillary Malformation-arteriovenous Malformation Type 2: A Case Report and Review.
evidence.snippet: Telangiectasias were reported in 28 (22%) patients, and Bier spots were described in 20 (15.7%) patients.

nodes.description: Cerebral arteriovenous malformations and vein of Galen malformations occur within the CM-AVM fast-flow lesion spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of key signaling regulators of cerebrovascular development in vein of Galen malformations.
evidence.snippet: To elucidate the pathogenesis of vein of Galen malformations (VOGMs), the most common and most severe of congenital brain arteriovenous malformations, we performed an integrated analysis of 310 VOGM proband-family exomes and 336,326 human cerebrovasculature single-cell transcriptomes.

nodes.description: Heterozygous pathogenic variants in RASA1 or EPHB4 reduce signaling control of endothelial vascular development, including negative regulation of Ras signaling, creating systemic susceptibility to CM-AVM fast-flow lesions.
nodes.id: Germline RASA1 and EPHB4 loss of vascular Ras suppression

biological_processes: negative regulation of Ras protein signal transduction

evidence.reference_title: RASA1 mutations and associated phenotypes in 68 families with capillary malformation-arteriovenous malformation.
evidence.snippet: Capillary malformation-arteriovenous malformation (CM-AVM) is an autosomal-dominant disorder, caused by heterozygous RASA1 mutations, and manifesting multifocal CMs and high risk for fast-flow lesions.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of key signaling regulators of cerebrovascular development in vein of Galen malformations.
evidence.snippet: Rare, damaging transmitted variants were enriched in Ephrin receptor-B4 (EPHB4) (17.5-fold, p = 1.22 x 10-5), which cooperates with p120 RasGAP to regulate vascular development.

nodes.description: Multiple small capillary malformations are the most characteristic cutaneous finding and often prompt molecular testing.

evidence.reference_title: RASA1 mutations and associated phenotypes in 68 families with capillary malformation-arteriovenous malformation.
evidence.snippet: Capillary malformation-arteriovenous malformation (CM-AVM) is an autosomal-dominant disorder, caused by heterozygous RASA1 mutations, and manifesting multifocal CMs and high risk for fast-flow lesions.

nodes.description: RASA1-related disease can include Parkes Weber syndrome with limb overgrowth and high-flow arteriovenous shunting.
nodes.id: Parkes Weber syndrome or limb arteriovenous shunting

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the clinical and molecular findings in RASA1 capillary malformation-arteriovenous malformation.
evidence.snippet: RASA1-related disorders are vascular malformation syndromes characterized by hereditary capillary malformations (CM) with or without arteriovenous malformations (AVM), arteriovenous fistulas (AVF), or Parkes Weber syndrome.

nodes.description: Impaired Ras suppression and EPHB4-RASA1 vascular developmental signaling converge on abnormal ERK/MAPK signaling and blood vessel morphogenesis, promoting arteriovenous malformation and fistula formation.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of key signaling regulators of cerebrovascular development in vein of Galen malformations.
evidence.snippet: Mice expressing a VOGM-specific EPHB4 kinase-domain missense variant (Phe867Leu) exhibited disrupted developmental angiogenesis and impaired hierarchical development of arterial-capillary-venous networks, but only in the presence of a "second-hit" allele.

nodes.description: Lesion tissue can lose the remaining wild-type RASA1 allele, supporting a two-hit model in which inherited susceptibility is followed by focal allele loss in malformation tissue.
nodes.id: Somatic second-hit allele loss in focal lesions

biological_processes: negative regulation of Ras protein signal transduction

evidence.reference_title: RASA1 mutations and associated phenotypes in 68 families with capillary malformation-arteriovenous malformation.
evidence.snippet: The analysis of the tissue showed loss of the wild-type RASA1 allele.

nodes.description: Telangiectasias occur particularly in EPHB4-related CM-AVM2 and can overlap clinically with HHT.

evidence.reference_title: Capillary Malformation-arteriovenous Malformation Type 2: A Case Report and Review.
evidence.snippet: Telangiectasias were reported in 28 (22%) patients, and Bier spots were described in 20 (15.7%) patients.
